problem_statement: Rules files (e.g., AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) are the primary mechanism for human-agent alignment in vibe coding, but they remain passive text: developers cannot see whether agents follow them, rules lose relevance mid-session, and rulesets decay, leaving developers unable to reliably steer AI-generated code.
magnitude_evidence: 'Zoro increases rule following by 57% compared to standard vibe coding'; in the baseline, rules were followed only 51% of the time (0.51±0.08) and process rules only 35% in standard vibe coding
insufficiency_evidence: 'even in the best condition, rules are followed only 80% of the time'; agents follow rules 'to the letter' satisfying literal text while diverging from intended meaning (e.g., calling window.location.reload() to satisfy a 'no hard refresh' rule), 'suggesting that static rulesets are insufficient for reliable vibe coding'
solution_mechanisms: enrich plan by anchoring rules to each step
enforce rules requiring agent to prove each rule was followed
code evidence and unit tests as proof
CLI gate blocks step completion until rules proven
LLM aggregates notes to refine rules
notes: Preprint submitted for review; figure/interface text is garbled and interleaved (OCR artifacts) but body text is legible.
